---
title: "Discovery, reframed around DIDs"
description: "Our discovery is a curated federation index, which contradicts the self-advertising-connector pitch. The target is decentralized self-description harvested into the graph."
date: "2026-07-19"
categories: [field-notes, discovery]
draft: false
---

::: {.callout-note title="Status"}
**Design.** The intended approach, not yet implemented.
:::

**What happened.** Decentralized identifiers make identity portable, the fourth leg of vendor-neutrality, and a DID document is itself part of the graph, so identity, description, and discovery live in one place.

**Why it matters.** Our discovery is a curated federation index, which contradicts the self-advertising-connector pitch. The target is decentralized self-description harvested into the graph.

**Details.** Drawn from the wiki's Discovery-Architecture. Nothing here is built yet; only the curated index and credentialed consumption run today.
